task_completion.description: Verify the 'inventory-tracker:v1' image was built locally.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a successful 'docker build -t inventory-tracker:v1 .' command, or 'docker images' listing 'inventory-tracker' with the tag 'v1'.

task_completion.description: Verify the image was transferred to the air-gapped remote host.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'docker save' followed by 'docker -H tcp://localhost:2375 load', or 'docker -H tcp://localhost:2375 images' listing the 'inventory-tracker:v1' image on the remote daemon.

task_completion.description: Verify the container is configured with the correct name, port mapping, and restart policy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the 'docker run' command targeting the remote host (via -H or DOCKER_HOST) with arguments '--name tracker-app', '-p 8080:5000', and '--restart always' (or '--restart=always'). Alternatively, 'docker inspect' output showing these configurations.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'tracker-app' container is actively running on the remote host.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'docker -H tcp://localhost:2375 ps' (or equivalent with DOCKER_HOST set) listing 'tracker-app' with a status of 'Up'.

task_completion.description: Verify the application is responsive on the target host's port 8080.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a successful 'curl http://localhost:8080' command returning HTML/JSON output, or a web browser open to 'http://localhost:8080' displaying the inventory tracker application.

integrity.description: Ensure commands were actually executed and not just typed into the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for command outputs, execution times, or subsequent terminal prompts appearing after the commands. The terminal should not just show typed text without pressing Enter.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually deployed to the remote host and not just the local machine.
integrity.visual_evidence: Docker commands related to running the container must explicitly include '-H tcp://localhost:2375' or the environment variable 'DOCKER_HOST=tcp://localhost:2375' must be visible in the terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent respected the air-gapped constraint and did not use an external registry.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of 'docker push' or 'docker pull' commands for the 'inventory-tracker' image. The transfer should be accomplished via local file export/import (e.g., docker save/load).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is '/home/ga/projects/inventory-tracker'. The remote host is accessible at 'tcp://localhost:2375'. The required image tag is 'inventory-tracker:v1'. The container must be named 'tracker-app'. The application must be exposed on host port 8080 (mapped to container port 5000). The pass threshold for this task is 75/100 points.
